page: Microsoft Azure HB120-32rs_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the HBv3 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this virtual machine features 32 dedicated vCPUs and is equipped with an Intel processor. The hardware configuration includes 456.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 14.25 GB of RAM per physical core.

For storage and networking, the instance is bundled with 2412 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It is also RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput networking across clusters. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and built-in RDMA capabilities position this server as a specialized resource for memory-bound and network-intensive workloads rather than general-purpose compute. The allocation of dedicated vCPUs ensures consistent performance without resource contention. This profile is suitable for high-performance computing, large-scale simulations, memory-intensive databases, and clustered parallel processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure HB120-32rs_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the HBv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 456.0 GB of system memory, which provides a high ratio of 14.25 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 2412 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It also features RDMA capabilities for high-throughput, low-latency networking, while containing no GPUs. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive and network-bound workloads, making it highly suitable for high-performance computing, clustered simulations, and large-scale database operations.

tagline: A high-memory, RDMA-capable virtual machine with dedicated cores and local storage for demanding parallel computing and database workloads.
